summary: Iteration 2 (Outlets + Restaurant Scanner + Outlet Gate) tested end-to-end. Backend: 10/10 pytest tests pass after fixing a critical tz-comparison bug in /api/scan/lookup and /api/scan/redeem. Frontend: /outlets (6 cards, city select, search), /outlets/:id (hero, deals list, address, map iframe, directions link, back button), /scan (mode toggle, manual student lookup with Verified badge, manual coupon lookup with Redeem button), Navbar shows Offers/Outlets/Scan for anonymous users, Landing shows 6 outlet cards + view-all link. Outlet gate verified: same-outlet second claim after redeem returns 409; unrelated outlet + non-outlet offers still claimable.

minor.issue: send_email() throws Resend errors ('You can only send testing emails to your own email address') in logs but is caught and returns False — non-blocking, just log noise.

issues: Hero image is cropped tightly on some outlets (e.g., Roastery & Co. shows only table-leg region because aspect-[16/7] + object-cover on a portrait-oriented photo). Consider object-position or curated landscape covers.

action_items: Review the tz-aware fix I applied in /app/backend/server.py (added _aware() helper; wrapped 3 comparisons in scan_lookup/scan_redeem). Consider applying the same helper everywhere MongoDB datetimes are compared to datetime.now(timezone.utc) to prevent future regressions (e.g., password_resets expiry check on line 323 also compares naive DB dt to aware — currently passes because pymongo returns naive but should be normalised for safety).
Optional: fix Resend FROM domain or gate send_email() by env to reduce log noise in dev.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED by tester: scan_lookup + scan_redeem crashed with TypeError('can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes') on every coupon/student lookup because MongoDB returns naive UTC datetimes but code compared them with datetime.now(timezone.utc). Introduced _aware(dt) helper and wrapped 3 comparisons.

Same pattern latent risk at server.py line 323 (password_resets expiry) — comparing doc['expires_at'] (naive from DB) with datetime.now(timezone.utc). Works currently by luck of comparison semantics on some Python versions? Actually it will raise TypeError if a reset is ever attempted. Recommend normalising with _aware() there too.
Outlet gate logic (server.py 605-621) uses this_created <= last_created — both naive from DB, so safe. Good.
seed_offers count check runs before seed_outlets, so outlet offers won't block generic seed. Ordering is correct.
server.py is 1281 lines — approaching the 700-line split threshold. Consider splitting into routers (auth, offers, outlets, scan, verification).
@app.on_event('startup') is deprecated in FastAPI — migrate to lifespan handlers.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@savycampusdeals.in / Admin@123. Fresh students created via /api/auth/register using @example.com emails (email-validator rejects .test TLD).
seed_data_creation: 6 outlets + 8 outlet-linked offers + 13 non-outlet SEED_OFFERS auto-seeded on backend startup. Multiple test students created during pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fixed: naive-vs-aware datetime comparison in scan endpoints. If further scan/coupon-expiry regressions appear, check the _aware() wrapper is still applied. Test users created with @example.com only (.test TLD is blocked by email-validator).
rca_of_the_issue: RCA: MongoDB (BSON) stores datetimes as naive UTC. When Python code does `db_dt < datetime.now(timezone.utc)` a TypeError is raised because one side is naive and the other is tz-aware. Repro: POST /api/scan/lookup with any coupon code → 500. Mitigation: introduced _aware(dt) helper which promotes naive dt to UTC-aware, then applied to expires_at/verification_expiry comparisons in scan_lookup and scan_redeem. Verified via pytest (10/10) and UI (Scan → manual coupon lookup → Redeem visible).
